date_notes: Canadaland (R-106) confirms the 2015 directive but does not pin the date. The 2015 federal election was October 19; endorsement editorials would have run in the days before. Original date 2015-08-19 was unsourced; updated to 2015-10 (month precision). The same practice was formalised in 2019 via the Kevin Libin appointment — a separate event may be warranted for the 2019 escalation.
title_en: National newspaper chain directs papers to endorse governing party
title_fr: Une grande chaîne de journaux dirige ses journaux vers l'appui au parti au pouvoir
description_en: Reporting confirms Postmedia leadership directed its newspapers to run editorial endorsements of the federal Conservative Party in the 2015 election. The Ottawa Citizen editorial board resigned the following month. A 2019 Canadaland investigation documented the same practice as a formally centralised directive, with Kevin Libin appointed as "executive editor of Postmedia politics" with a mandate to make the chain "reliably conservative." Paul Godfrey (Postmedia executive chair) identified as a longtime Conservative donor. Documented as a media-ownership event with ongoing structural implications.
verification_notes: Handoff notes multiple Tier-2 sources exist but no single URL captured. Attach before publication.

update_log.change: Added R-106 (Canadaland, Aug 2019). Date updated 2015-08-19 → 2015-10 (month). Description expanded to note 2019 formalisation. Original unsourced date noted in date_notes.
